stages.name: Normalize
stages.description: Text cleaning + 31 abbreviation expansions + URL/mention stripping

cards.title: Customer Data References in Open Channels
cards.description: Cluster of 4044 documents containing customer-specific data references (IDs, issues, data handling). Detected across 30 products. Risk: customer PII and issue details in unsecured communication channels.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 2566 messages, 0 transcripts, 23 docs.

sample_messages: Sounds good, George. One more thing, in the Security and Authentication section, should we mention any specific compliance standards we're aiming to meet, like GDPR or CCPA? This could be important fo
I agree with @eid_8658e19c. Their NLP capabilities are impressive, but I wonder how they handle privacy concerns with all that data processing. 🤔

TensorFlow's focus on model training efficiency is interesting. While our primary goal is ingestion, their methods for handling data efficiently could be beneficial. We might find some useful patterns
First up, we have Mattermost's integration of webhooks for third-party app interactions. This feature allows Mattermost to exchange data in real-time with other applications. You can check out the PR

Thanks, Julia! The Mattermost approach seems pretty straightforward and could be a good reference for setting up our endpoints. Real-time data exchange is crucial for us too. I think we should definit

sample_snippets: Sounds good, George. One more thing, in the Security and Authentication section, should we mention a
I agree with @eid_8658e19c. Their NLP capabilities are impressive, but I wonder how they handle priv
cards.caveats: Cluster confidence: 95%. Based on TF-IDF similarity — verify semantic relevance.
cards.recommendation: Implement DLP scanning on Slack channels. Route customer-specific discussions to private channels with access controls.

cards.title: API/Integration Work Siloed Across Teams
cards.description: 1745 documents about API development and integration architecture. Each product team building API layers independently without shared standards or components.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 1328 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

sample_messages: Great points, both of you! Next, let's look at Rocket.Chat's real-time event API integration. This feature facilitates seamless interactions with external applications. Here's the PR link: https://git

Thanks for the input, everyone! I'll take a closer look at these features and see how we can adapt some of their strategies for our Slack Events API integration. I'll keep you all updated on my findin
Alright, I've gone through the changes. The Slack Events API integration looks solid, and I can see that the endpoints are correctly set up to handle incoming events. Nice work! 👍

sample_snippets: Great points, both of you! Next, let's look at Rocket.Chat's real-time event API integration. This f
Thanks for the input, everyone! I'll take a closer look at these features and see how we can adapt s
cards.caveats: Cluster confidence: 95%. Based on TF-IDF similarity — verify semantic relevance.
cards.recommendation: Establish API governance council. Create shared API design guidelines and reusable SDK components across product teams.

cards.title: Pull Request Discussion Outside Review Tools
cards.description: 3510 PR-related discussions happening in Slack instead of in the PR review tool. Decisions and context lost outside the code review system.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 3231 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

cards.title: Hi Team / Team
cards.description: Cluster of 989 documents sharing phrases: hi team, hi, team, open source, source. Found across 30 products and 1 source types.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 989 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

cards.title: Informal Approval Patterns (LGTM Culture)
cards.description: 797 instances of informal approval language ('LGTM', 'approved') without formal review workflows. Approvals happen in Slack rather than through structured review systems.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 797 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

sample_messages: Everything looks good to me. LGTM, approved! ✅
Yep, I can confirm that too. The integration looks seamless. LGTM, approved! ✅
Great to hear! I just went through the code and the documentation. Everything looks solid to me. The alerting system is well-configured, and the error handling is comprehensive. LGTM, approved! 👍

sample_snippets: Everything looks good to me. LGTM, approved! ✅
Yep, I can confirm that too. The integration looks seamless. LGTM, approved! ✅
cards.caveats: Cluster confidence: 95%. Based on TF-IDF similarity — verify semantic relevance.
cards.recommendation: Implement required approval workflows in CI/CD. LGTM in Slack should trigger formal review tool approval, not replace it.

cards.title: Positive Sentiment Clustering (Baseline)
cards.description: 12194 documents with positive/forward-looking language. Serves as sentiment baseline — deviation from this pattern could indicate team morale issues.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 10948 messages, 36 transcripts, 49 docs.

Good point, Emma. Also, in the Market Overview section, it might be helpful to include some recent statistics or trends about the growth of AI in collaboration tools. This could strengthen our argumen
I agree with George. Adding some data could really bolster the Market Overview. Additionally, in the Target Audience section, maybe we could specify more about the size of businesses we're targeting.
Sounds good, @eid_fd8cecea. Looking forward to it! 👍
Looking forward to it! Thanks for the great discussion, everyone. 😊

sample_snippets: Thanks for sharing, Charlie! @eid_13fdff84 I think the Executive Summary is clear, but it might bene
Good point, Emma. Also, in the Market Overview section, it might be helpful to include some recent s
cards.caveats: Cluster confidence: 95%. Based on TF-IDF similarity — verify semantic relevance.
cards.recommendation: Use as baseline for sentiment monitoring. Track ratio of positive vs. concern-bearing messages over time.

cards.title: Information Sharing Patterns
cards.description: 4644 documents with information-sharing language. Healthy communication baseline indicating active knowledge transfer.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 4644 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

cards.title: Request/Response Communication Patterns
cards.description: 1024 question-and-response exchanges. Normal operational communication — useful as baseline for measuring response time patterns.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 1024 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

cards.title: Channel Join/Leave Churn
cards.description: 891 channel join/leave events detected. High channel membership turnover may indicate unclear channel purpose or team restructuring.
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 891 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

cards.title: Open Collaboration Signals
cards.description: 590 instances of open-invitation language ('feel free to...'). Indicates collaborative culture but may mask unclear ownership — who is actually responsible?
cards.effect: Pattern spans 30 product teams with 590 messages, 0 transcripts, 0 docs.

sample_messages: Absolutely, feel free to reach out if you need a hand with anything. Looking forward to the updates! 😊
Absolutely, feel free to reach out if you need anything. Thanks for tackling this important update, @eid_4cede092! 🙌
Yeah, feel free to reach out if you need a hand. Once the preferences are sorted, I think we'll be good to go! 🚀

sample_snippets: Absolutely, feel free to reach out if you need a hand with anything. Looking forward to the updates!
Absolutely, feel free to reach out if you need anything. Thanks for tackling this important update,
cards.caveats: Cluster confidence: 95%. Based on TF-IDF similarity — verify semantic relevance.
cards.recommendation: Pair 'feel free' invitations with explicit ownership: 'Feel free to review, but Alice owns the final decision.'
